title: ExtensionAPI host declaration is an empty stub, forcing blind `as unknown as X` casts at every Pi entry point
evidence: pi-api.d.ts declares `export interface ExtensionAPI {}` (no members) for `@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent`. Every consumer of the real `pi` object then does `pi as unknown as ToolGuardPiPort`, `pi as unknown as ParentPiPort`, `pi as unknown as ToolResultPiPort` (extension.ts:428,429,436,457,478,501,502,514,537) or the even weaker `pi as never` (extension.ts:441, feeding installPiCompaction). child-extension.ts:209 does the same with `pi as unknown as ChildPiPort`.
impact: The TypeScript compiler never checks that the locally-defined Port interfaces (ToolGuardPiPort, ParentPiPort, ToolResultPiPort, ChildPiPort) actually match what Pi 0.80.x's real ExtensionAPI exposes. A Pi version bump that renames/removes `registerCommand`, `getActiveTools`, etc. produces a silent runtime TypeError instead of a compile error; `pi as never` at line 441 additionally suppresses all excess-property and argument-count checking for the compaction call, so a shape mismatch there is invisible even at the call site.
recommendation: Either declare `ExtensionAPI` in pi-api.d.ts as the real union of the locally-used Port interfaces (so `pi: ExtensionAPI` satisfies ToolGuardPiPort/ParentPiPort/etc. structurally, without `as unknown`), or introduce one narrow, audited adapter function that performs a single runtime shape-check (duck-typing assertion) and returns the typed ports, replacing the ad hoc `as unknown as X` / `as never` casts scattered across call sites.
acceptance_criteria: pi-api.d.ts's ExtensionAPI declares the members actually consumed (on, registerCommand, sendUserMessage, getCommands, registerTool, getActiveTools, getAllTools, etc.) so downstream casts reduce to plain type narrowing without `unknown`
no remaining `pi as never` cast in extension.ts
a version-mismatch of Pi's real API surface is caught by `tsc` rather than only at runtime
